channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: The war on Iran will likely end in American retreat
item.description: The American empire cannot win the war against Iran at acceptable financial, military, and political costs.

item.title: Senior Sri Lankan monk arrested for alleged child sex crimes
item.description: Pallegama Hemarathana, the chief prelate of Colombo, is accused of abusing an 11-year-old girl in a temple.

item.title: Hacked educational platform partially restored for millions of students
item.description: The hacker group, ShinyHunters, threatened to leak student data after breaching the educational platform Canvas.

item.title: Russia holds downsized Victory Day parade
item.description: Putin invoked the Soviet victory in World War II to rally his army in Ukraine, saying 'our heroes move forward'.

item.title: Spain’s Canary Islands brace for incoming hantavirus-stricken cruise ship
item.description: 140 passengers and crew on the MV Hondius vessel will be 'completely isolated' and evacuated, say Spanish authorities.

item.title: Israeli settlers set fire to homes and cars in violent West Bank raids
item.description: Dozens of Israeli settlers stormed various areas of the West Bank, set cars on fire, and attacked Palestinians.

item.title: Financial struggles burden Yemen’s army as soldiers wait for wages
item.description: Yemeni soldiers earn $38 to $116 monthly, but an unstable currency has eroded the value of their salaries.

item.title: Wembanyama powers Spurs past T-Wolves as Knicks beat Sixers in NBA playoffs
item.description: San Antonio take 2-1 semifinal series lead over Minnesota, while New York Knicks are 3-0 up against Philadelphia 76ers.

item.title: Cambodians struggle with displaced lives amid tense ceasefire with Thailand
item.description: Families displaced by fighting fear new clashes with Thailand, say education among areas most affected by border war.

item.title: Iran war live: US expects Tehran’s reply to peace deal; ‘clashes’ in Hormuz
item.description: Israel killed 31 people in southern Lebanon on Friday, including a rescue worker, Lebanon's National News Agency said.

item.title: Pentagon releases video of strikes on Iranian oil tankers
item.description: Footage released by the Pentagon shows US strikes on two Iranian oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Celebrated naturalist David Attenborough marks 100th birthday
item.description: Britain’s famed natural historian David Attenborough is celebrating his 100th birthday.

item.title: Costa Rica inaugurates right-wing president Laura Fernandez
item.description: Costa Rica inaugurated right-wing president Laura Fernandez on Friday as supporters gathered in the national stadium.

item.title: Laura Fernandez sworn in as Costa Rica’s new president
item.description: Fernandez enters office with her right-wing party holding an absolute majority in the legislature.

item.title: Venice Biennale: The Art of the Unseen
item.description: At this year’s Venice Biennale, the story isn’t just about what’s on display—but what’s missing.

item.title: Could Labour and Conservative party dominance in UK politics be ending?
item.description: Britain's prime minister is under pressure to quit after huge losses in local elections.

item.title: Al Jazeera reporter witnesses Israeli strikes on Lebanon
item.description: Israel has increased its attacks across southern Lebanon. Al Jazeera’s Obaida Hitto reports from Tyre.

item.title: Protests in the Canary Islands as virus-stricken ship heads for port
item.description: Protesters in Tenerife are voicing opposition to the imminent arrival of a cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak

item.title: Libya’s largest oil refinery halts operations during fighting
item.description: Zawiya refinery shut down in 'precautionary measure' as emergency declared following explosions and gunfire nearby.

item.title: Four convicted in US related to killing of Haitian President Jovenel Moise
item.description: Prosecutors charge Florida served as central hub in 2021 assassination of Moise, which set off ongoing political crisis.

item.title: Trump announces three-day ceasefire in Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Truce will also include swap of 1,000 prisoners of war from each country, US president says.

item.title: ASEAN leaders adopt measures to ease economic pain caused by Iran war
item.description: The bloc currently imports more than half of its crude oil from the Middle East.

item.title: Olympic water polo resignations cite lack of respect, belittling behaviour
item.description: Olympic and European holders in mass resignation, saying Serbia chief has shown a lack of respect for their efforts.

item.title: How and when can Arsenal or Man City win the Premier League?
item.description: Manchester City face Brentford on Saturday, while Arsenal travel to West Ham on Sunday in red-hot Premier League race.

item.title: More than a dozen reported killed in Israeli attacks on south Lebanon
item.description: Israeli air raids on southern Lebanon escalate ahead of new round of talks expected next week in Washington, DC.
